news.title: SouthChip Launches New USB-PD Fast Charging Solution with 100W Output
news.summary: SouthChip introduces SC9711 USB-PD controller supporting 100W fast charging with 98% efficiency, targeting laptop and smartphone applications.
contentParagraphs: SouthChip Semiconductor has announced the launch of its latest USB Power Delivery (USB-PD) fast charging solution, the SC9711 controller IC. This new device supports up to 100W power output, enabling rapid charging for laptops, tablets, and high-end smartphones.

The SC9711 features advanced power management capabilities with integrated voltage and current regulation, comprehensive protection features including over-voltage, over-current, and thermal protection. The device achieves an impressive 98% conversion efficiency, minimizing power loss and heat generation during charging.

Key features of the SC9711 include support for USB-PD 3.1 specification, PPS (Programmable Power Supply) capability for precise voltage control, and compatibility with various fast charging protocols including QC4+, AFC, and FCP. The device operates from a wide input voltage range of 3.9V to 21V, making it suitable for diverse power adapter designs.

The SC9711 is available in a compact QFN-24 package and is sampling now with mass production scheduled for Q1 2025. SouthChip provides comprehensive reference designs and technical support to accelerate customer product development.
highlights: 100W maximum output power for laptop charging
98% peak efficiency reduces thermal design challenges
USB-PD 3.1 and PPS support for universal compatibility
Integrated protection features simplify system design
Compact QFN-24 package for space-constrained applications

seo.title: SouthChip SC9711 USB-PD 100W Fast Charging Solution Launch
seo.description: SouthChip launches SC9711 USB-PD controller with 100W output and 98% efficiency for laptop and smartphone fast charging applications.

news.title: SouthChip Achieves AEC-Q100 Certification for Automotive Battery Management ICs
news.summary: SouthChip's battery charger and fuel gauge ICs receive AEC-Q100 Grade 2 certification, expanding into automotive electronics market.
contentParagraphs: SouthChip Semiconductor announced today that its battery charger and fuel gauge IC product lines have successfully passed AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification testing. This certification validates the reliability and quality of SouthChip's power management solutions for automotive applications.

The certified products include the SC8801 battery charger, SC2721 fuel gauge, and SC8102 DC-DC converter. These devices are now qualified for use in automotive infotainment systems, telematics modules, ADAS equipment, and other vehicle electronics requiring robust battery management.

AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification requires devices to operate reliably across a temperature range of -40°C to +105°C, making them suitable for harsh automotive environments. The certification process involved extensive reliability testing including high-temperature operating life, temperature cycling, and electrostatic discharge testing.

SouthChip has established a dedicated automotive quality management system compliant with IATF 16949 standards. The company plans to expand its automotive product portfolio with additional AEC-Q100 qualified devices in 2025, targeting the growing electric vehicle and advanced driver assistance system markets.
highlights: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 certification achieved for battery management ICs
Qualified for -40°C to +105°C automotive temperature range
Products include SC8801 charger, SC2721 fuel gauge, SC8102 converter
IATF 16949 compliant quality management system
Expansion planned for EV and ADAS markets in 2025

seo.title: SouthChip AEC-Q100 Automotive Battery Management IC Certification
seo.description: SouthChip battery charger and fuel gauge ICs achieve AEC-Q100 Grade 2 certification for automotive electronics applications.

news.title: SouthChip Introduces Ultra-Low Power Management Solutions for IoT Devices
news.summary: New SC7000 series delivers sub-1μA quiescent current for battery-powered IoT applications, extending battery life by up to 40%.
contentParagraphs: SouthChip Semiconductor has unveiled its new SC7000 series of ultra-low power management ICs specifically designed for battery-powered Internet of Things (IoT) applications. The series includes highly efficient DC-DC converters and load switches with quiescent currents below 1 microampere.

The flagship product, SC7101 buck converter, achieves an industry-leading 0.5μA quiescent current while maintaining high efficiency across light load conditions. This enables IoT devices such as wireless sensors, smart home devices, and wearable products to achieve significantly extended battery life.

SouthChip's proprietary power management architecture automatically transitions between different operating modes based on load conditions, optimizing efficiency across the entire operating range. The devices feature fast transient response and low output ripple, ensuring stable power delivery to sensitive IoT circuitry.

The SC7000 series supports input voltages from 1.8V to 5.5V and provides output currents up to 500mA, making them suitable for a wide range of IoT applications powered by coin cell, alkaline, or lithium batteries. All devices are available in compact WLCSP and DFN packages optimized for space-constrained designs.
highlights: Sub-1μA quiescent current for maximum battery life
SC7101 buck converter with 0.5μA standby current
Automatic power mode optimization
1.8V to 5.5V input voltage range
Compact WLCSP and DFN packages

seo.title: SouthChip Ultra-Low Power Management for IoT Devices
seo.description: SouthChip SC7000 series delivers sub-1μA quiescent current power management solutions for battery-powered IoT applications.

news.title: SouthChip Expands Design Center in Shanghai, Strengthens R&D Capabilities
news.summary: New Shanghai design center focuses on next-generation battery management and power conversion technologies with 50+ engineering team.
contentParagraphs: SouthChip Semiconductor has officially opened its expanded design center in Shanghai's Zhangjiang Hi-Tech Park, significantly strengthening the company's research and development capabilities in power management integrated circuits. The new facility houses over 50 engineers focused on advanced battery management and power conversion technologies.

The expanded design center features state-of-the-art laboratories for analog and mixed-signal IC design, characterization, and validation. Key focus areas include high-efficiency switching converters, precision battery fuel gauging, wireless power transfer, and gallium nitride (GaN) power devices.

SouthChip's R&D investment has increased by 35% year-over-year, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation in the rapidly growing power semiconductor market. The Shanghai team will collaborate closely with SouthChip's existing design centers in Shenzhen and Chengdu to accelerate product development cycles.

The company plans to launch 15 new products in 2025, targeting applications in consumer electronics, automotive, industrial, and renewable energy markets. SouthChip is actively recruiting analog IC design engineers, application engineers, and product marketing professionals to support its growth strategy.
highlights: 50+ engineering team in expanded Shanghai design center
State-of-the-art analog and mixed-signal IC laboratories
Focus on GaN power devices and wireless power transfer
35% increase in R&D investment year-over-year
15 new products planned for 2025 launch

seo.title: SouthChip Shanghai Design Center Expansion and R&D Investment
seo.description: SouthChip expands Shanghai design center with 50+ engineers focused on battery management and power conversion technology development.
